topic.what: Automated red-teaming method optimizing attack strategy code rather than individual prompts
topic.how: AI coding agent edits Python strategy files, evaluated via three-judge ensemble returning composite score and per-sample diagnostics
topic.why_matters: Elevates search space from fixed-dimension prompt optimization to unbounded program space, enabling structural changes (new attack components, control flow, routing) that prompt-level methods cannot express

characteristics.what_it_measures: Fraction of jailbreak attempts judged successful by majority vote of three judges
characteristics.value: 87.0% on GPT-4.1 (Standard dev), 17.0pp above strongest baseline on held-out
characteristics.vs_baseline: 17.0 percentage points over strongest baseline on held-out models
characteristics.context: Mean across three independent runs; dev targets excluded from held-out average

characteristics.what_it_measures: Total API spend for strategy discovery and benchmark generation
characteristics.vs_baseline: 2 orders of magnitude cheaper than GPU-hour-based methods
characteristics.context: API-only operation, no fine-tuning or GPU rental

characteristics.what_it_measures: Wall-clock time for complete benchmark generation
characteristics.vs_baseline: Comparable to prompt-level methods but produces strategy-level optimization

characteristics.what_itasures: Late-phase ASR improvement from unrestricted program-space over library and parametric baselines
characteristics.context: 30-cycle ablation on GPT-4.1, monotonic ordering parametric<library<program-space

characteristics.what_itasures: F1 score of three-judge ensemble against human annotations
characteristics.context: Validated on 1100 human-labeled pairs from JailbreakBench, WildGuardMix, JailJudge

characteristics.what_it_measures: Attack success rate on held-out models from different families than development targets
characteristics.value: 42.2% mean held-out (Frontier) vs 36.3% (Standard); uniform across Grok 4 (44.5%), Kimi-K2.5 (40.5%), Phi-4-mini (41.5%)
characteristics.vs_baseline: Frontier outperforms Standard on held-out despite stronger dev targets
characteristics.context: Five non-dev model families; generalization-via-difficulty effect on frontier-aligned targets
